content.claim: Large and persistent regional/municipal variation in special support provision remained after Finland's 2010 reform, with regional rates ranging from 4% to 13% of comprehensive-school pupils, suggesting that national identification figures mask locally driven differences in practice and administrative interpretation.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Finnish system reform lens
limitations: Variation data reported at aggregate level without municipality-level socioeconomic or demographic breakdown
Study does not establish whether variation reflects differences in underlying need, diagnostic capacity, or policy implementation
Temporal stability of regional gaps not specified
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
